title: Eat That Frog!
author: Brian Tracy
core_idea: Stop procrastinating and get more done by tackling your most important task first thing in the morning. Tracy provides 21 practical techniques to overcome procrastination and maximize productivity.
key_concepts: Your 'frog' is your biggest, most important task — the one you're most likely to procrastinate on.
If you have to eat a frog, do it first thing in the morning.
If you have to eat two frogs, eat the ugliest one first.
The key to success is action — start before you feel ready.
Planning every day in advance saves hours of execution time.
notable_quote: If you have to eat a live frog, it does not pay to sit and look at it for a very long time.
practical_lessons: Identify your most important task and do it first every day.
Apply the 80/20 rule — focus on the 20% that produces 80% of results.
Break large tasks into smaller, manageable pieces.
Develop the habit of completing tasks before moving on.
